title: Create audit-foundry skill
summary: Create a foundry-owned, skill-first ECC audit workflow that writes Markdown reports for foundry-only, foundry-plus-projects, projects-only, and specific-project audit scopes. It audits organization, tracker consistency, active skill surfaces, validation behavior, generated UI state, open workflow tasks, and project state as required by the selected report type. Project integration and connection registration are deferred to a later task.
acceptance_criteria: the task defines the operator job for auditing whether the foundry is organized before any implementation artifacts are created
project integration and connection registration are explicitly out of scope for this task and will be handled later

the implementation is skill-first and aligned to ECC concepts including workflow contract, input contract, output artifact, eval gate, human boundary, handoff, reviewer lane, and project state preload
the primary callable surface is a Codex-accessible .agents/skills skill with matching SKILL.md frontmatter and agents/openai.yaml metadata
the audit skill slug is audit-foundry
the audit-foundry skill reads the selected scope state and explains the current system, open work, risks, and recommended next actions so the operator knows what to do
the audit-foundry skill supports multiple report types: foundry-only, foundry-plus-projects, projects-only, and specific-project
the audit-foundry skill defaults to foundry-only unless the operator explicitly asks for projects or names a specific project
audit-foundry invocation defaults to $audit-foundry for foundry-only scope
audit-foundry supports explicit project-inclusive scopes with scope:foundry-projects and scope:projects
audit-foundry supports specific-project reports with project:<slug>
foundry-only reports audit the workflow-foundry project and foundry control plane
foundry-plus-projects reports audit the workflow-foundry project plus all selected or discoverable project trackers
projects-only reports audit project trackers without foregrounding foundry control-plane analysis
specific-project reports audit one named project and its non-done task state
the audit-foundry skill is read-only against foundry source state: it does not fix, rewrite, reclassify, or mutate tasks, skills, project files, registry entries, validation scripts, or generated UI
the audit-foundry skill writes a Markdown audit report artifact that captures the state of the foundry, explains findings, and names recommended next tracker actions
audit-foundry stores reports neatly under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/reviews/ with date-stamped audit-foundry Markdown filenames
audit-foundry always writes the Markdown audit report, then summarizes the report in chat
audit report writes are output artifacts, not state fixes; any follow-up changes must happen through a later initiate-task or continue-task flow
slash-style operator access is provided through the Codex skill surface, not through legacy commands or prompt shims
skill structure follows Matt Pocock skill-writing guidance: predictable steps, concise trigger description, progressive disclosure for references, and no duplicated no-op prose
the future skill distinguishes canonical active skills from project artifacts, tracker files, generated UI, testing sessions, and quarantined imports

the future skill audits the foundry, including root instructions, registry, workflow-foundry tracker state, skill surfaces, validation scripts, and generated UI state when the selected report type includes foundry scope
the future skill audits project state, project AGENTS.md boundaries, project.json, task index, non-done tasks, and testing-session indexes when the selected report type includes project scope
the future skill reports foundry and project organization gaps as candidate tracker tasks instead of silently editing unrelated surfaces
the future workflow preserves Matt Pocock presentation style and ECC workflow architecture

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, all non-done workflow-foundry tasks, existing foundry review/help skills, and Matt/ECC references before shaping the audit-foundry skill.

approved_artifacts.approval_note: Approved by user instruction to continue workflow-foundry-011 through implementation for the audit-foundry skill.
Approved by user instruction to expose audit-foundry as a Codex-accessible skill.
Approved to list audit-foundry in the canonical workflow help surface.
Approved to include audit-foundry in canonical skill invocation validation.
Approved to verify audit-foundry behavior by writing a real foundry audit report artifact.
Tracked as a real audit-foundry foundry-only report artifact created while verifying the audit skill.
Tracked as a real audit-foundry foundry-plus-projects report artifact created while verifying the audit skill.

session_log.event: Created intake task after user invoked initiate-task to create a skill that audits the foundry itself, keeps everything organized, and explicitly routes actual workflow search through the real-life-workflows project before integrating SME workflow material.

Moved to grilling after user selected workflow-foundry-011 and clarified the two required capabilities: a one-way project integration flow where the foundry labels and connects to projects while projects remain isolated, and a full foundry audit covering the foundry plus connections to integrated projects.

Resolved first grilling decision: integrated projects need a foundry-owned connection registry or equivalent foundry-owned connection record, and the eventual implementation must be skill-first and aligned to ECC workflow concepts.

Resolved skill surface decision: use the repo-recommended Codex-accessible .agents/skills pattern with SKILL.md plus agents/openai.yaml metadata, preserve slash-style operator access through Codex skills, and avoid legacy command or prompt shims.

Corrected skill split: the new callable skill should be audit-focused only. Project integration should be handled through the existing initiate-task flow when the operator explicitly says the task is for integration, rather than by creating a separate integrate-project skill.

Resolved audit skill identity and scope: the skill slug should be audit-foundry; it should read and explain the foundry so the operator knows what to do; it should support foundry-only and foundry-plus-projects scopes; and the default invocation should audit the foundry plus connected or integrated projects.

Resolved default connected-project preload: audit-foundry should read each connected project's project summary, task index, and every non-done task JSON by default, while keeping full artifacts, testing-session event streams, and quarantined import bodies on-demand unless exact evidence is needed.
Narrowed this task to audit-foundry only after user deferred project integration and connected-project audits to a later task.

Resolved audit output boundary: audit-foundry should remain read-only against foundry source state, but should create Markdown audit report artifacts that explain the state of everything in scope and identify recommended next tracker actions.
Resolved report storage: audit-foundry should store Markdown reports neatly under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/reviews/ using date-stamped audit-foundry filenames.
Resolved report creation mode: audit-foundry should always write the Markdown audit report, then summarize it in chat.

Expanded audit report modes after user clarified that audit-foundry needs multiple report types: foundry-only, foundry plus projects, projects-only, and specific-project reports. Project integration and connection registration remain deferred.

Resolved audit invocation behavior: default $audit-foundry should run foundry-only scope; use scope:foundry-projects for foundry plus projects, scope:projects for projects-only, and project:<slug> for a specific project report.
Wrote the audit-foundry PRD and moved the task to PRD phase with issues as the next action.
Published three local issue artifacts for the audit-foundry skill contract, audit report generation, and help/validation/review work; moved the task to issues phase with implement as the next action.
Moved to implement and approved audit-foundry skill files, workflow-help update, validation update, and a real foundry audit report artifact.

Implemented the audit-foundry skill and Codex metadata, updated workflow-help and validation, wrote a real foundry audit report artifact, and marked implementation issue criteria complete except final code-review.
Completed focused code-review/spec-review for audit-foundry. No blocking issues found in the skill contract, Codex metadata, help entry, validation hook, or generated foundry audit report.
Linked two real audit-foundry report artifacts created during foundry verification so validation can distinguish owned audit outputs from untracked planning files.
Closed during cleanup after audit-foundry implementation and focused code review completed with linked report artifacts.
